text: "This is Baba Yaga's curse, cast upon the Baron years ago:"
speaker: zara

text: "Upon von Spielburg and all his clan, this Curse I now demand:  What I will shall come full measure, so shall ye lose all that ye treasure."
speaker: zara

text: "There is always a way to break a curse; possibly Erasmus knows more about this one."
speaker: zara

text: "Baba Yaga's hut is Magical.  It stands on chicken legs, and you must know the rhyme to enter."
speaker: zara

text: "I have no interest in entering Baba Yaga's hut and therefore do not know the rhyme."
speaker: zara

text: "He was once the great leader of this land until he was foolish enough to anger Baba Yaga."
speaker: zara

text: "Erana was a powerful Spellcaster who lived long ago.  She brought peace to this valley.  Even now her spell protects this town from violence or foul Magic."
speaker: zara

text: "Her final resting place is due north of town, and it is a place of both safety and healing.  It is known as `Erana's Peace'"
speaker: zara

text: "Erasmus is a Wizard and a Spellcaster who knows much about this area.  He lives in a tower eastward of town.  He can be very helpful if approached properly, but he has a strange sense of humor."
speaker: zara

text: "It is a steep mountain to the northeast."
speaker: zara

text: "That is the Spell that lets you cast a Magical Flame upon your enemy.  The Spell Scroll will cost you 60 silver."
speaker: zara

text: "That is the Spell that lets you place magical energy upon a weapon.  The energy will be released as magical damage when you hit your enemy."
speaker: zara

text: "It is a Spell that allows you at first to unfasten simple locks and knots.  As you gain in skill, then you may open doors with it.  This Spell Scroll will cost you 30 silver."
speaker: zara

text: "The Fetch Spell allows you to lift or lower a lightweight object from a distance.  The Spell Scroll will cost you 40 silver."
speaker: zara

text: "We sell several Magical Spells on study scrolls.  You may purchase Flame Dart for 60 silver, Fetch for 40 silver, or the Open spell for 30 silver."
speaker: zara

text: "The Scrolls we sell are Magical.  You have but to read the Spell, and you will learn it."
speaker: zara

text: "I also know that you can find a wonderful Spell if you learn the secret of Erana's Peace."
speaker: zara

text: "Secrets are made to be kept."
speaker: zara

text: "We can sell you spell learning scrolls and enchanted potions."
speaker: zara

text: "The Healing Potion will cost you 50 silver.  It is made by the Healer outside of town."
speaker: zara

text: "Vigor Potions restore your Stamina after you have exerted yourself.  They cost 25 silver each."
speaker: zara

text: "Power is the essence of magic, that which the wizard shapes and is shaped by."
speaker: zara

text: "The Power Potion restores your magical energy, and costs 75 silver."
speaker: zara

text: "We have Healing, Vigor, and Power Potions."
speaker: zara

text: "Master the Arts Arcane, use those skills to vanquish the evil curse, and you will become a true Hero!"
speaker: zara

text: Just look at the shelves.
speaker: narrator

text: "We are entities of Magic and Power.  Matters such as that are of little importance."
speaker: zara

text: "We choose only to answer questions which interest us.  Do not persist with uninteresting questions."
speaker: zara

text: "We shall not waste our time further."
speaker: zara

text: "You may choose from the Fetch, Open, or Flame Dart Spells."
speaker: zara

text: "You may choose from the Healing, Vigor, or Power Potions."
speaker: zara

text: "I do not sell such things to such as you."
speaker: zara

text: Zara ignores you.  You would do better if you showed some respect by walking up to the counter before talking to her.
speaker: narrator

text: "So, you return to my shop.  Ask of me what you will, but keep to the point."
speaker: zara

text: "I am Zara, and my companion is Damiano."
speaker: zara

text: "The items in this shop are designed for those skilled in the use of magic.  We have very little for ones such as you who have not been initiated, but what we have could prove useful."
speaker: zara

text: This dark, seedy place is less than you expected of a Thieves' Guild.
speaker: narrator

text: The man seated by the table greets you with a scowl;  Crusher is his usual, amiable self;  and you can't quite make out what lurks behind that massive door.
speaker: narrator

text: You feel uneasy about the sinister mood here.
speaker: narrator

text: "I don't have time to talk to unlicensed scum.  Pay your guild license fee, and then we can talk."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "What do you think this is, your home?  Crusher, deal with this deadbeat."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: The door is locked, and Boris won't let you in.
speaker: narrator

text: Picking locks in the Thieves' Guild is a singularly stupid idea.
speaker: narrator

text: Crusher ignores you.
speaker: narrator

text: "A Thief's license will cost you 25 silvers.  You will need a license to use any of our services."
speaker: boris

text: "Perhaps I could sell you a good lockpick for fifteen silvers, or a wonderful Acme Thief Tool Kit for one hundred silvers."
speaker: boris

text: "I have no time for idle talk.  Why don't you do something constructive like stealing Otto's yo-yo?"
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "I can't hear you.  Please come a little closer to the window."
speaker: boris

text: From his expensive clothes to his carefully trimmed mustache, there is an air of sophistication to this villain.
speaker: narrator

text: Crusher is his usual, amiable self (every bit the Goon).
speaker: narrator

text: The opening in the door is covered with a wire grate, but there is space below it where objects can be passed through.  The mysterious-looking man on the other side is Boris, the cashier and fence for the Guild.
speaker: narrator

text: The ladder up to the tavern is very sturdy.  It has to be to support Crusher's weight.
speaker: narrator

text: It looks like an oversized, well-used dartboard.
speaker: narrator

text: There sure are a lot of daggers in the board.  You wonder where the Chief Thief keeps them all.
speaker: narrator

text: The shadowy figure behind the door is Boris, the cashier and fence for the Guild.
speaker: narrator

text: The floor is surprisingly clean, compared to that of the bar upstairs.
speaker: narrator

text: The plaster is chipping in several places.
speaker: narrator

text: You can tell where the bar is by the seep marks of spilled beer.
speaker: narrator

text: The daggers are nicely made, and apparently designed for throwing.  Besides the Chief Thief's seemingly endless collection, there are two matched sets with differently-colored hilts for playing Dag-Nab-It.
speaker: narrator

text: Even thieves get thirsty.
speaker: narrator

text: You're probably wondering if there is any honor among thieves.
speaker: narrator

text: "It will cost you 25 silvers to work in town.  Just pay Boris at the door.  If you don't have the cash, go steal something."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: You reach for your money but come up empty-handed.  Unable to pay for your goods, you put the merchandise back.
speaker: narrator

text: This house is the same color as the Sheriff's office around the corner.
speaker: narrator

text: A simple but prosperous-looking edifice it is.  It looks like it might provide some staples that would prove useful to a traveler.
speaker: narrator

text: It is but a small stall, used for the daily sale of whatever local produce has not been confiscated by the brigands.
speaker: narrator

text: There is no response.  The store must be closed for the evening.
speaker: narrator

text: There is no one around.  The markets are closed for the night.
speaker: narrator

text: All of the produce has been taken in for the night.
speaker: narrator

text: The creature in charge of the Farmer's Mart is a rather lovely and shy-looking young centaur.
speaker: narrator

text: The selection is meagre and the appearance is not of the best, but the aromas are strangely enticing.  A barrel contains some small apples, and there are several types of vegetables on the counter.
speaker: narrator

text: This appears to be a Market street.  In the corner is a fruit and vegetable stand, or Farmer's Mart.  Next to it is a dry goods store, and next to that is a house.  Across the street you see the back of the barber's shop and the Sheriff's office.
speaker: narrator

text: You see the backs of buildings.
speaker: narrator

text: Unlike an Ogre, YOU can read!
speaker: narrator

text: The lock appears to be an extra heavy-duty model.
speaker: narrator

text: The door appears to be locked, but the lock is of a simple type.
speaker: narrator

text: "I will be happy to take your silver.  Please pick out what you want."
speaker: hilde

text: "As you wish, sir."
speaker: hilde

text: The pretty young centaur looks at you and says, "Good day, and welcome to Spielburg!  Do you wish to buy some nice fresh fruits or vegetables?"
say: Good day, and welcome to Spielburg!  Do you wish to buy some nice fresh fruits or vegetables?
speaker: hilde

text: "I am Hilde, daughter of Heinrich Pferdefedern, the farmer."
speaker: hilde

text: "My father and I are the only centaurs in this valley."
speaker: hilde

text: "We have many kinds of fresh vegetables for sale today.  There are lettuce, carrots, potatoes, and turnips right from the ground at five for a silver.  They are very good for you!"
speaker: hilde

text: "It is much too early in the season for those.  Sorry."
speaker: hilde

text: "I'm sorry, but the cherry harvest is not yet in.  But we will have very nice cherries two months from now."
speaker: hilde

text: "There are still some apples left from last season in the barrel.  You may buy ten for a silver, since they are so small.  We will have fresh cherries in a couple of months."
speaker: hilde

text: "They have twice robbed my Father of his money, and they have tried to steal our food this winter.  That is because we farm outside of town.  They would not dare to try that in town.  The Sheriff would stop them fast!"
speaker: hilde

text: "Many robbers have run up to Father as he trotted back to town.  My Father tried to fight, but they hurt him badly.  They always ran away to the west."
speaker: hilde

text: "We have some land to the north of town.  It isn't very big, but we grow many things.  You should be here during the harvest time.  Then you would see many fruits and vegetables."
speaker: hilde

text: "You know...in the autumn!  But then, perhaps you have never been a farmer."
speaker: hilde

text: "The Healer lives in a nice little cottage on the North road, near my Father's fields.  She is a very nice woman."
speaker: hilde

text: "Thank you, but my Father thinks I am too young.  He would not permit it.  But perhaps next year..."
speaker: hilde

text: "My Father is Heinrich Pferdefedern, who works our farm just north of town."
speaker: hilde

text: "I am sorry.  I do not get about very much, and I do not really know about many things."
speaker: hilde

text: If you want to buy something, go over to the vendor.
speaker: narrator

text: You pick out the freshest-looking vegetables you can find, and pay the lady a silver piece.
speaker: narrator

text: You carefully select ten of the best apples from the barrel, and pay the lady a silver piece.
speaker: narrator

text: "As I told you, sir, it is too early in the season.  You would not want to buy any."
speaker: hilde

text: "I'm afraid I can't help you with that, sir."
speaker: hilde

text: "Just pick out what you would like to purchase, then you can pay me."
speaker: hilde

speaker: narrator

text: The small apples give off a sweet but pungent odor.

text: Carrots are good for night vision and lower one's cholesterol.

text: You can smell apples as you approach this corner.
speaker: narrator

text: However, this was not a good place to sleep.  You're stiff and sore all over and your wallet feels very light.  You give the Hilde the centaur a suspicious look, but you are too sore to pursue the matter.
speaker: narrator

text: "I'm sorry, but I had to move you out of my stall!  It is very dangerous to sleep in the street at night.  I fear you have been robbed."
speaker: hilde

text: The smell of lavender and dust fills your nose as you walk in.  This reminds you of a great aunt's house you once visited.  The couch looks every bit as uncomfortable as your aunt's.
speaker: narrator

text: There is a covered birdcage near the stairs, and a knitting basket beside the couch.

text: You find nothing else of value in the desk.
